summary: This cluster investigates the molecular physiology of dinoflagellates, specifically focusing on their complex signaling and ion transport systems. Through transcriptomic analysis of 27 samples, researchers reconstructed a consensus channelome comprising 36 ion channel families, revealing homologs of both animal and plant proteins alongside unique protist-specific channels. The findings highlight the organisms’ remarkable physiological adaptability, driven by extensive G-protein coupled receptor and calcium-dependent signaling pathways. This work establishes a foundational genomic resource for understanding how these marine protists integrate environmental cues, though the geographic scope of the analyzed transcriptomes was not specified.
